---
label: "Context Loading"
icon: "\uD83D\uDCDA"
---

## Context Loading & CLAUDE.md

The quality of AI-generated code depends almost entirely on the <JargonTip term="context window">context</JargonTip> you provide. Context engineering for code means giving the <JargonTip term="agent">AI agent</JargonTip> the right files, patterns, and constraints.

  <CodeBlock b64="" />
  <ExpandableSection title="Context Hierarchy for AI Coding" icon="📚" defaultOpen>

      - **CLAUDE.md / .cursorrules:** Project-level conventions, stack, patterns
      - **Relevant files:** Similar existing code the AI should follow
      - **Type definitions:** Interfaces, schemas, API contracts
      - **Test patterns:** How existing tests are structured
      - **Error examples:** The specific error you{"'"}re debugging with stack trace

  </ExpandableSection>
  <Quiz question="What is the #1 factor that determines the quality of AI-generated code?" options={["The model size (GPT-4 vs GPT-3.5)","The quality and relevance of context provided to the model","The programming language used","How detailed the prompt is"]} correctIndex={1} explanation="Context is king. A well-contextualized request to a smaller model will produce better code than a vague request to a larger model. Giving the AI your conventions, similar files, types, and patterns lets it generate code that actually fits your codebase while staying within token limits."/>
